title: Delevar
content:

{{Quote|If you ''ever'' do anything like that again…make sure you ''tell'' me first. Nice going, kid.|Bren Derlin to Delevar after the latter used a smoke canister to defeat an Imperial scout walker|<ref name="Night Attack">{{WEGCite|story=Night Attack|book=Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi}}</ref>}}
[[Corporal/Legends|Corporal]] '''Delevar''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Trained as a [[Medic/Legends|medic]] and a [[Scout/Legends|scout]], he served with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine's]] [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|SpecForces]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], he was part of the force assigned to the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command's]] [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the ice [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Surviving the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Delevar regrouped with the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]], and remained stationed with the armada for a [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Alliance learned that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was constructing a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]], and decided to [[Battle of Endor/Legends|strike]] at the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] while it was still being built. However, the half-completed [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] was protected by an energy [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] generated from a [[Endor shield generator bunker|facility]] on the surface of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. A [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]] was organized to infiltrate the moon, and deactivate the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]]. Delevar volunteered to be part of the team; although he was considered to be a rookie [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]], the unit commander, he was highly recommended by General Madine.

Delevar attended the briefing aboard the Alliance [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], during which Madine laid out the intended plan of attack, and he later traveled to Endor on board the stolen Imperial [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']]. Following an encounter with an Imperial [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] patrol, Delevar and the strike team separated from the command crew led by the former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], General [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Following orders to rendezvous with the general at 0300, the strike team avoided Imperial patrols until they encountered an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]] while making camp for the night. Delevar managed to subdue the walker with a clean shot into its open [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] with a [[Smoke canister/Legends|smoke canister]] fired from a [[CSPL-12 projectile launcher/Legends|Caspel launcher]] that forced the crew to abandon the vehicle. Delevar then participated in the ground engagement of the Battle of Endor, which saw the destruction of the shield generator that subsequently allowed Alliance [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to destroy the Death Star.

==Biography==
===Service on Hoth===
The [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="Endor-Delevar" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="Delevar" /> Delevar served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|SpecForces]] as a [[Medic/Legends|medic]] and [[Scout/Legends|scout]], and held the rank of [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]]. He was assigned to [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]],<ref name="Endor-Delevar" /> the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] headquarters on the ice [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> and participated in the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]].<ref name="Endor-Delevar" /> Despite [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces attaining a decisive victory over the Alliance,<ref name="ESB" /> Delevar survived the battle, and managed to escape the planet.<ref name="Endor-Delevar" /> Following the battle, he was stationed with the main [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]].<ref name="GG5">''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]''</ref>

===Briefing and infiltration===
{{Quote|We have stolen a small Imperial shuttle. Disguised as a cargo ship, and using a secret Imperial code, a strike team will land on the moon and deactivate the shield generator.|General Crix Madine briefs the assembled Rebels aboard ''Home One''|audio=Madine-Imperialshuttlestolen.flac|<ref name="RotJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>}}
[[File:STooVees-ROTJ.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Delevar (third from left, front row) attends the briefing on the Endor mission aboard ''Home One'']]
[[4 ABY/Legends|One year later]],<ref name="NEC2">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] take place in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> Delevar volunteered to be part of a [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]] being assembled by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Although considered to be a rookie [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] by the team's planet-level unit [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]],<ref name="GG5" /> Delevar was highly recommended by General [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], commander of the Alliance's SpecForces.<ref name="Strike Team">{{FactFile|44|END 11-14|The Battle of Endor}}</ref> The team was being put together for a mission to disable an Imperial [[Endor shield generator bunker|facility]] on the surface of the [[Endor/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]]. This facility projected a powerful energy [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] that protected the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], which was undergoing construction in orbit of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. Having learned of its location through the efforts of the [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]]—and the fact that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] would be aboard during the final construction phases—the Alliance decided to [[Battle of Endor/Legends|strike]] at the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. However, for any attack to succeed, the shield would have to be deactivated before they arrived.<ref name="RotJ" />

Delevar attended a briefing aboard the Alliance [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']] along with the rest of the [[Commando/Legends|commandos]], sitting in the front row of the semi-circular amphitheater, where General Madine outlined the basic operation the team would undertake. They would infiltrate the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] using a stolen Imperial [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T4 shuttle]]—the [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']]—and a secret Imperial code that would clear them through the military picket lines.<ref name="RotJ" />

Armed with standard commando gear—including jungle fatigues and pack, standard [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], [[Low-feedback scanner/Legends|low-feedback scanner]], [[Sensor scrambler (equipment)|sensor scrambler]], [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinoculars]], and a heavily muffled [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]—Delevar joined the rest of the unit on the ''Tydirium''. Also carried by the team was an [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web heavy repeating blaster]], and a [[CSPL-12 projectile launcher/Legends|Caspel launcher]].<ref name="GG5" /> The ''Tydirium'', [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by General Solo and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] copilot [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], left the Alliance Fleet in the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]], and proceeded directly towards the Endor system. Arriving at their destination, the shuttle was queried by the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the moon. The stolen Imperial code allowed them passage through the military lines—although they were unaware that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], had [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] the presence of his son, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], aboard the shuttle and had allowed them to pass on purpose.<ref name="RotJ" />

===Landing on Endor===
{{Quote|Take the squad ahead. We'll meet at the shield generator at 0300.|General Han Solo to Major Derlin|audio=SoloDerlin.flac|<ref name="RotJ" />}}
[[File:Brenderlin.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Major Bren Derlin, Delevar's commanding officer]]
Landing in a forest clearing, General Solo began to lead the strike team toward their objective. Shortly after moving out, the team came upon an Imperial [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] patrol<ref name="RotJ" /> consisting of [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Elsek]]<ref name="Endor-Elsek">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Sergeant Elsek}}</ref> and Corporal [[Avarik]].<ref name="Endor-Avarik">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Corporal Avarik}}</ref> Although Solo and Chewbacca attempted to take out the patrol quietly, Avarik was alerted to Solo's presence when the Alliance general stepped on a twig. Backhanding Solo, Avarik yelled for<ref name="RotJ" /> his<ref name="Avarik's Databank">{{DB|character|avarik|Avarik, Corporal}}</ref> partner to go for help. Elsek mounted<ref name="RotJ" /> his<ref name="Elsek's CCG">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Sergeant Elsek}}</ref> [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] and attempted to flee, but was subsequently killed when Chewbacca destroyed his vehicle with several shots from his [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]]. However, the commotion alerted another pair of scout troopers, and they shot off into the forest on their own speeder bikes. Skywalker, along with [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]—who had volunteered to be part of the mission's command crew—took Avarik's bike and gave chase.<ref name="RotJ" />

Delevar, along with the rest of the squad, waited at the location where they had encountered the initial patrol until Skywalker returned. When it became clear that Skywalker and Organa had gotten separated during the chase, and that the princess had not returned with the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Solo decided to take the command crew and search for her. Mindful of the mission, however, he ordered Major Derlin to continue on, and to rendezvous at the target at 0300.<ref name="RotJ" /> Derlin led the squad toward the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]], aware that more Imperial patrols lay between them and their objective. Several times the squad encountered a patrol and managed to hide; however, Delevar almost compromised their position by preparing to take a potshot at a speeder bike. Derlin managed to stop him in time, and upbraided the commando for his reckless action.<ref name="Night Attack" />

At nightfall, Derlin ordered the squad to make camp in a shallow ravine, well protected by underbrush. Posting two sentries and setting up a [[blanket grid]] with the scanning equipment, the squad settled down to rest. Shortly after starting the first sleeping shift, the scanners picked up a large target moving toward them. Derlin suspected that the contact was an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]]; his suspicions were proved correct when its twin spotlights were spotted in the distance. Ordering the squad to take cover, he hoped that the AT-ST would pass them by. When it became clear that it would not, Derlin ordered Corporal [[Dansra Beezer]],<ref name="Night Attack" /> the squad's communications expert and [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]], to jam its transmissions.<ref name="Endor-Beezer">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Corporal Beezer}}</ref> [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Greeve (Human)|Greeve]], the squad's sharpshooter, knocked out both of the spotlights with precise shots<ref name="Night Attack" /> from his [[A280 blaster rifle/Legends|A280 blaster rifle]].<ref name="Endor-A280">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=A280 Sharpshooter Rifle}}</ref> To keep the scout walker off balance, Derlin ordered the squad to circle it, although he was afraid that the walker pilot would simply start blasting away at the forest now that his vehicle was blind and deaf.<ref name="Night Attack" />

True to Derlin's fears, the scout walker began firing randomly into the trees and foliage. Delevar, aware that the battle could alert other Imperial forces in the area to their presence, grabbed the squad's Caspel launcher and loaded a [[Smoke canister/Legends|smoke canister]] into it. Moving into a trench in front of the walker, Delevar took careful aim at the face of the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]. Derlin—believing that the launcher contained an explosive round, and fearing that the resulting explosion would ''definitely'' tip the Imperials off to their location—shouted at Delevar to stop. Knowing exactly what he was doing, the commando ignored the order and fired the [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] straight through the cockpit window. The resulting explosion of smoke in the cockpit forced the walker pilots to abandon the vehicle, surrendering with tears streaming from their eyes. The squad captured the pilots, and forced them to make a comm check-in at blasterpoint. With this accomplished, Derlin approached Delevar; the major affected a growl, and told the commando if he ''ever'' did anything like that again, he was to tell him first. Then he slapped Delevar on the shoulder and congratulated him on a job well done.<ref name="Night Attack" />

===Victory on the forest moon===
{{Quote|The rest, as they say, is history.|Bren Derlin, about the Battle of Endor's outcome|<ref name="Night Attack" />}}
[[File:EndorBunker-ROTJ.png|thumb|left|300px|The back door of the shield generator bunker on Endor]]
Delevar and the squad continued on to the rendezvous point, forcing the walker pilot to make several more check-ins on the way to explain the scout walker's absence.<ref name="Night Attack" /> At 0300, the squad met up with Solo and the command crew, who had entered into an alliance with a native tribe of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], the moon's indigenous species. After an initial reconnaissance of the target, the Ewok scouts informed General Solo and the strike team that there was a secret entrance to the bunker on the other side of the ridge. Although the entrance was guarded by four scout troopers, one of the Ewoks, [[Paploo/Legends|Paploo]], stole one of the guards' speeder bikes. Three of the four troopers gave chase, leaving one behind to guard the door. General Solo and the strike team took the trooper prisoner, and subsequently infiltrated the bunker to plant their explosives.<ref name="RotJ" />

However, the entire mission had been designed by the Emperor himself as a trap for the Alliance. He had allowed the Alliance to gain intelligence on the Death Star and the location of the shield generator, and had arranged for an entire [[Legion/Legends|legion]] of troops to be on the moon.<ref name="RotJ" /> Delevar<ref name="Night Attack" /> and the strike team were captured by Imperial forces laying in wait, both inside and outside the bunker. With the shield still active, the incoming Alliance Fleet could not assault the Death Star's [[Hypermatter reactor/Legends|reactor core]] as planned, forcing them into a protracted engagement with a large Imperial fleet led by the Star Dreadnought ''Executor''.<ref name="RotJ" />

Delevar<ref name="Night Attack" /> and the strike team were rescued by the Ewok tribe the command crew had befriended. Retaking the offensive, the Rebels and the Ewoks engaged the Imperial forces in battle around the bunker. During the fight, Chewbacca managed to commandeer an AT-ST, and Solo used its communications system to fool the bunker personnel into opening the back door with a fake message claiming that the Rebel forces had been routed. Able to access the bunker once more, Solo and the strike team planted [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton grenades]] in the structure. The resulting explosion destroyed the shield generator installation, which in turn allowed Alliance [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to destroy the Death Star by destroying its reactor core.<ref name="RotJ" />

==Personality and traits==
Although an experienced soldier, Delevar was considered to be a "rookie" by the other members of the strike team, including Major Derlin,<ref name="Strike Team" /> an opinion that was reinforced when he almost blew the team's cover on the Endor mission by taking a potshot at a speeder bike.<ref name="Night Attack" /> Despite this, he was known to pride himself on being an efficient soldier in General Madine's commando organization;<ref name="Endor-Delevar" /> he was calm when facing an AT-ST, and had enough tactical sense to know that a smoke canister would disable the crew of the scout walker without causing a large commotion.<ref name="Night Attack" />

==Skills and abilities==
Like all the commandos assigned to the strike team, Delevar had extensive training in the use of blasters and explosives.<ref name="Endor-Training">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Commando Training}}</ref> He was also trained in the use of a Caspel launcher,<ref name="Night Attack" /> and was an experienced medic.<ref name="Endor-Delevar" />

==Equipment==
Delevar and the rest of the Endor strike team were equipped with basic commando gear, which included a standard [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a low-feedback scanner, a [[Sensor scrambler (equipment)|sensor scrambler]], a [[Sound suppressor|heavily muffled]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], and full-[[Forest/Legends|forest]]-camouflage [[Alliance military uniform/Legends|fatigues]].<ref name="GG5" />

==Behind the scenes==
Delevar first appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] as an unnamed commando during the briefing scene aboard ''Home One''.<ref name="RotJ" /> In ''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]'', published in [[1990]], a commando named Delevar appeared in the short text story ''[[Night Attack]]''.<ref name="Night Attack" /> It was not until the [[1999]] release of the "''[[Endor Limited]]''" expansion for the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] that the unnamed commando from the briefing was identified as being Delevar.<ref name="Endor-Delevar" />

''Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi'' establishes that Delevar is a rookie at the time of the Battle of Endor.<ref name="Night Attack" /> However, his ''Star Wars Customizable Card Game'' entry from the ''Endor Limited'' set states that he is a veteran of the Battle of Hoth.<ref name="Endor-Delevar" /> ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]''{{'s}} entry on the strike team asserts that while Delevar was an experienced soldier, he was still considered to be a rookie by the rest of the team.<ref name="Strike Team" />

According to [[Leland Chee]]'s blog on [[StarWars.com]], he speculated that [[Decipher]] used pictures of the same actor to represent Delevar and Sergeant [[Brooks Carlson/Legends|Brooks Carlson]] on their respective CCG cards.<ref name="Chee blog">{{Blog|holocron|26|General Solo's Rebel strike team}}</ref> Carlson was portrayed by stuntman [[Alf Joint]], who was uncredited in the role.<ref name="Aficionado">{{Blogspot|subdomain=starwarsaficionado|url=2010/09/classic-image-rebel-assembly.html|name=Star Wars Aficionado|text=CLASSIC IMAGE: REBEL ASSEMBLY!}}</ref>
